q: The objective of an astronomical telescope has a large focal length fo and the eyepiece has a small focal length fe.
In a camera, the diaphragm is placed in front of the convex lens.
The image formed on the film of a camera is enlarged.
Myopia is the defect of vision in which the eye can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant objects clearly.
The crystalline lens of the eye is a diverging lens.
The image formed in the film by a camera is virtual.
A simple microscope consists of a single converging lens of short focal length.
For a normal human eye, the near point is about 25 cm.
In a compound microscope, the first image is inverted.
Hypermetropia is corrected using a converging lens.
The smaller the f-number, the narrower the aperture is.
The image formed by a simple microscope is virtual, erect and magnified.
In cameras, the images can only be recorded on the films.
An astronomical telescope consists of two converging lenses.
In an astronomical telescope, the first image is erect.
The eyepiece of a compound microscope is a converging lens of short focal length.
The focal length of the objective lens is smaller than the focal length of the eyepiece lens in a compound microscope.
All cameras form the images in the same basic way.
The retina acts as a screen on which the image is formed.
In a simple microscope, the object is placed at the focus of the lens.
When a telescope is in normal adjustment, the final image is formed at infinity.
In a camera, the diaphragm is a circular opening through which the light passes.
The magnifying power of a telescope in normal adjustment is M = fo / fe.
In a compound microscope, the final image is inverted with respect to the first object.
When taking a photograph in bright light a small f-number must be used.
Spectacles are optical instruments employing lenses.
The eye is very similar to a camera in its optical function.
In a compound microscope, the first image is real, inverted and magnified.
The size or the diameter of the aperture is expressed in terms of the f-number.
Many different types of lenses play an important part in our daily lives.
The magnifying power of a simple microscope is always less than 1.
In a compound microscope, the first image must fall within the focal length of the eyepiece.
A compound microscope consists of two converging lenses.
The camera uses a concave lens to form an image.
In an astronomical telescope, the objective lens has a short focal length.
Hypermetropia is the defect of vision in which the eye can see distant objects clearly but cannot see nearby objects clearly.
When photographing in dim light a narrow aperture must be used.
In a compound microscope, the final image is virtual.
The image formed on the film of a camera is erect.
Myopia is corrected using a diverging lens.
In a compound microscope, the object is placed between Fo and 2Fo of the objective lens.
The focal length of the objective lens is much larger than that of the eyepiece lens in an astronomical telescope.
In an astronomical telescope, the first image is real and inverted.
The magnifying power of a simple microscope is M = 1 + D/f.
The lens near the object in a compound microscope is called the eyepiece.
The separation between the two lenses of an astronomical telescope in normal adjustment is L = fo + fe.
When photographing in bright light a wide aperture must be used.
A person with a myopic eye can see nearby objects clearly.
In a camera, the diaphragm controls the amount of light energy falling on the film.
The image formed on the retina is real and inverted.
